sectionHeadings: 020: Derived-artifact merge driver and merge-queue serialization
record.summary: This repo commits two deterministic derived artifacts, registry.json and index.json, each carrying a global content hash. Two branches that both regenerate them conflict textually on the hash line, and rebasing onto a freshly merged PR forces a regenerate-and-recommit cycle. This spec adds an opt-in, per-clone git merge driver (spec-spine-derived-regen) that resolves such a conflict by regenerating both artifacts from the merged working tree and handing the fresh bytes back to git, plus a one-command registrar and the committed .gitattributes path assignments. It also turns on the GitHub merge queue (the merge_group event) for the build/test and determinism workflows so every queued PR is validated against the speculative merged tree; the coupling gate stays pull_request-only because it reads the PR-body waiver, which the merge_group event does not carry. The driver is a convenience over the conflict and is never a substitute for the index-staleness gate (spec 004), which remains the freshness source of truth.
record.title: Derived-artifact merge driver and merge-queue serialization
